risinfo.chaptertitle: Appendix II. Test Your Knowledge! Answer Key

sect1.title: Chapter 15

listitem.para: a. True. For ICD-10-CM and CPT coding purposes, the day of birth is day zero (0) of life and the next day is day 1. Monday is day 1 of life for the newborn delivered on Sunday.

d. Perinatal conditions are considered clinically significant if the condition has implications for future health care needs or any of the clinical indications for reporting codes from other chapters (ie, requires clinical evaluation, therapeutic treatment, diagnostic procedures, extended hospital stay, or increased nursing care or monitoring).
b. A single normal newborn care code is reported. Codes 99460–99463 are reported once per date of service regardless of the number of visits or time spent.

d. 99026 (hospital-mandated on-call service; in hospital, each hour) is reported in addition to the initial hospital care of the normal newborn (99460). Attendance at delivery (99464) is not reported when hospital-mandated attendance is the only basis for providing the service.

sect1.para: 5. c. If the circumcision with a clamp or other device is performed without dorsal penile or ring block, append modifier 52 (reduced services) to 54150.
